+- Verified: `cd kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run src/components/grid-view` â†’ 5/5 pass. `npx tsc --noEmit` â†’ clean.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-04-16 17:48)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx:564,109` â€” Guard inconsistency: `useGridData` reduces invalid `view.entity_type` values to `""` via `VALID_ENTITY_TYPE`, but `GridView`'s guard only checks `!view.entity_type` (truthiness of the raw view field). A view with a non-empty-but-invalid `entity_type` (e.g. `"Foo Bar"`, uppercase, or containing `:`) will bypass the "missing entity_type" branch and render an `AddEntityBar` / keyboard shortcuts that dispatch `entity.add:` â€” which the backend correctly rejects but surfaces as a confusing user-facing failure. Fix: gate `AddEntityBar` (and the `grid.newBelow` / `grid.newAbove` commands) on `entityType !== ""` (the sanitized value), or make the early-return in `GridView` check the sanitized `entityType` instead of `view.entity_type`.
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx:344,354,521` â€” The grid hard-codes `title: \`New ${entityType}\`` as an override for every entity type, but `AddEntity` silently drops overrides for fields not declared on the entity schema (see `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/entity/add.rs:181-189`). For `tag` (field `tag_name`) and `project` (field `name`), the title override is discarded and the new entity gets the schema-default label ("new-tag", "New item"). The acceptance criterion "creates a new entity of the correct type" is met, but the grid's "New task"/"New tag"/"New project" intent is only honored for task. Either (a) stop passing the `title` override and rely on the schema default in all three call sites, or (b) look up the entity's title-ish field from the schema and pass the override under its real name.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx:343-347,353-357,520-524` â€” Three copies of the same `dispatch(\`entity.add:${entityType}\`, { args: { title: \`New ${entityType}\` } }).catch(...)` call now live in this file. Extract a single `addNewEntity(dispatch, entityType)` helper (or a local closure in `GridView`) and call it from both keyboard command execs and the `AddEntityBar` click handler.
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.test.tsx` â€” No regression test asserts that `grid.newBelow` / `grid.newAbove` (the `o` / `O` / `Mod+Enter` keyboard commands that were changed from `${entityType}.add` to `entity.add:${entityType}`) actually dispatch the new command id. The button click test covers the new code path but the keyboard path was the whole point of step 2 of the task and has no lock-in. Add a test that invokes the `grid.newBelow` CommandDef's `execute` (either via the command palette, a keypress, or by reaching through the CommandScope) and asserts `mockInvoke` received `cmd: "entity.add:task"`.
\ No newline at end of file
